title: Battle of Kiev
summary: The Battle of Kiev was the largest encirclement in military history. German Army Group South, reinforced by Guderian's Panzer Group 2 swinging south from Army Group Centre, encircled four Soviet armies east of Kiev in a pocket that yielded over 660,000 prisoners.
background: Hitler's controversial decision to divert Guderian's Panzers south toward Kiev rather than continuing toward Moscow created the conditions for the encirclement. Guderian and the Army General Staff opposed the decision; Hitler overruled them, and the Kiev operation produced the war's most devastating single encirclement.

axis.strength: Army Group South + Guderian's corps from Army Group Centre

outcome: Catastrophic Soviet defeat. 665,212 prisoners taken — the largest single encirclement in history. The four Soviet armies of the South-West Front were effectively destroyed. General Kirponos was killed attempting to break out.

casualties.soviet: ~616,000–700,000 killed, wounded, or captured; 3,718 guns, 884 tanks lost
